mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount factors, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods with a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, which supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 205.0, inflationary pressures trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 205.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength, reducing recession risk and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with classification accuracy below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 60-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.
